task_completion.description: The agent opens the edit configuration screen for the 'nginx-web' deployment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows an 'Edit Deployment' or 'Edit Config' screen for the 'nginx-web' deployment.

task_completion.description: The agent updates the container image to the new version in the edit form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The container image input field in the Rancher UI is filled with 'nginx:1.26-alpine'.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the deployment configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'Save' and the UI transitions back to the deployment details or list page, often showing an 'Updating' state.
The deployment status in the Rancher UI shows as 'Active' with the expected number of pods running, and no pods are stuck in a terminating or pending state.

task_completion.description: The deployment or its pods reflect the updated image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The deployment details page, container specs, or individual pod details explicitly display the image as 'nginx:1.26-alpine'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Rancher web interface to perform the upgrade.
integrity.visual_evidence: All actions are performed within the web browser showing the Rancher UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a terminal to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows or command-line interfaces (like running 'kubectl set image') are visible in the screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the live Rancher application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show realistic UI states, such as dropdowns, form fields, and loading indicators, rather than a text editor or static mockups.
